segments.text: [MUSIC PLAYING]

KIRUPA CHINNATHAMBI:
Hi, everyone.

Today, we're going to learn
about how Firebase Studio can

help you build high-quality
full-stack AI apps quickly.

My name is Kirupa Chinnathambi,
and I'm a PM on Firebase Studio.

I'm joined by my
colleague Rody Davis,

who is a developer relations
engineer also on the same team.

We're going to start by getting
a 30,000-foot overview of how

app development has changed and
why we built Firebase Studio.

We will follow up with a deeper
look at Firebase Studio's

marquee features, along with a
live demo that really highlights

the breadth and depth
of capabilities that
can help you be productive.

So there are three big
changes to app development

over the years that
played a big role in us
building Firebase Studio.

First, app development has
gotten really difficult.

Building apps today is time
consuming, often frustrating,

and filled with numerous
hoops we need to jump through.

And this complexity spans
the full software development
lifecycle.

All stops on this path
are more complicated
now than they've ever been.

This complexity is
particularly frustrating

if you're a
professional developer.

You and I don't have the luxury
of skipping steps or cutting
corners.

Some of the complexities
you need to wrangle

include answering what your
technology stack should be.
What are your dependencies?

Is what you're building secure,
scalable, and performant?

Depending on your project,
your team, and the industry

you're working in, the types
of questions you need to answer
can be numerous and varied.

All of this can be
hugely overwhelming.

The software
development lifecycle

should be smoother and
easier for all of us

to navigate as we
build our apps.

A trend that we've been
observing for some time, that

really accelerated in
the last few years,

is that software
development is becoming

more decentralized and
collaborative across devices,
geographies, and time zones.

It used to be that all we
needed to get our apps built

was our primary, often
powerful, computer.

Now, over time, we
started to take our work
onto our personal devices.

These could be our
laptops, phones, tablets,

and an even smaller
devices like watches.

Beyond just building our apps
across a variety of devices,

we also now work from
a variety of locations.
It could be a coffee shop.

It could be a
secondary workplace.
It could even be our homes.

To add more color
to the situation,

we now collaborate closely
with other people, each of whom

could be working
on the same project

from different locations,
different devices, and maybe
even different time zones.

Third, and one that is probably
very top of mind for all of us,

is the role AI assistants
and powerful LLMs

are playing in
completely changing how
we as developers build apps.

This visualization really drives
home the two extreme workflows

you, me, and our
teams are likely
adopting when building apps.

On one end, we
have a world where

we are in the driver's
seat, and the AI
plays a purely assistive role.

On the other end,
we have a world

where the AI is doing almost
all of the heavy lifting.

We're playing more
of a consulting role

and nudging the AI into the
right direction if needed.

The complexity of
problems AI can solve,

though, has grown
by leaps and bounds.

Today, a quarter of all new code
at Google is generated by AI.

Beyond just
productivity, use of AI

also tends to increase overall
satisfaction and happiness.

Here's where a study that showed
individuals assigned to use AI

were both productive
and more happy

than individuals and teams
who didn't use AI at all.

This chart highlights
what I said a second ago,

where the use of AI
pretty much leads

to an increase in
positive emotions

and a decrease in
negative emotions.
This is pretty powerful.

In recognizing these three major
shifts, a handful of us who

love developer tools got
together a few years ago
and asked, can we do better?

We landed on a
handful of principles.

First, the ideal environment
needs to be ubiquitous.

You should be able to build
any app from any device,
from anywhere.

You and I should
have easy access

to integrated solutions,
not individual puzzle pieces

that we have to figure out how
to combine in the right way.

AI assistants shouldn't just
be limited to coding scenarios
or tacked on as an afterthought.

AI assistance needs to
be thoughtfully woven

throughout the entirety of the
software development lifecycle.

Now, software
development has always
been a collaborative activity.

And we need to ensure
that our tools help here,

where we can be working across
a variety of devices, locations,
and time zones.

To help answer the question
of "Can we do better?,"

we're thrilled to
announce Firebase Studio.

Firebase Studio is an integrated
and extensible agentic workspace

to build, run, and
manage web apps,

cross-platform mobile apps,
backend services, and more.

Let's walk through some
of the high-level features

that Firebase Studio
brings to the table.
First, it is fully web-based.

All you need is a browser
and an internet connection.

And just because
it's in a browser,

though, does not mean you have
to sacrifice capabilities.

You have a full
Cloud VM with access

It's also built on the
familiar VS Code environment.

You have easy starting
points, configurable using

AI assistance and
collaborative features

Now, Firebase Studio may look

It is built on the learnings

We actually went public

So it's great to highlight

Now, let's take a look

and walk through its
most common features.

When you launch Firebase
Studio for the first time,

you'll have a bunch
of options on how

You can start with our
app prototyper, where

you specify what
kind of app you'd

In this mode, you can
ask us to build an app.

Once you do that,
we first translate

your request into something

Think of a blueprint as a

When you're OK with
the requirements,
we start building your app.

You can continue to interact

You can draw directly

You can select elements

you would like to see.

Once you are ready to share

you can choose to deploy

available with our preview mode.

Now, going back to the
Firebase Studio landing page,

you can also create
a new workspace

We provide some of the
most common templates

available to you for

You can explore
our full collection

the most common starting

points for frontend,

If you want to go one level

we give you a lot of

how your environment is

build tools, and more.

You can also bring your existing

You can choose to open your

from your local computer.

Independent of how
you get started

with building your app
in Firebase Studio,

we give you full
agentic capabilities

throughout with
our AI assistants.

Changes you ask for can be

This is the power of Gemini

Beyond our built-in AI systems,
agents from Gemini Code Assist

Our goal is to ensure

You can use a lot of AI,

Lastly, we talked about the

Firebase Studio also share

Before I hand it

a main takeaway is that Firebase
Studio is a professional tool

Whether you're AI first, code
first, frontend, backend,

we have you covered.
All are welcome.
Now let's turn it over to Rody.
RODY DAVIS: Hi, my name is Rody.

So now it's time to see

There are two main

Firebase Studio has a powerful
